title_original: Quantum effect could power the next generation of battery-free devices
summary_original: A study led by Professor Dongchen Qi (Queensland University of Technology) and Professor Xiao Renshaw Wang (Nanyang Technological University) demonstrates that the nonlinear Hall effect in a topological quantum material can convert alternating current signals directly into direct current without magnetic fields or diodes. Published in Newton (2026), the research shows the effect remains stable at room temperature, with voltage direction and strength controlled by temperature-driven changes in the material’s imperfections and lattice vibrations. This mechanism could enable self-powered sensors and ultra-fast components for wireless networks, highlighting a practical path for quantum effects to move from theory to real-world, battery-free device applications.
